LAW OFFICES
OF
WAGGONER & LOEFFLER
318 McHENRY AVENUE
MODESTO, CALIFORNIA 95354

CARL O. WAGGONER
MICHAEL G. LOEFFLER

TELEPHONE (209) 544-7144
FAX (209) 544-7151

SUSAN E. BURNS

January 13, 1994

Deanne Stone
Counsel, Legal Division
California Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95804-0807

Re: Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No.: 2870
Your File No.: I-90-529

Dear Ms. Stone:

Last week you asked for additional information regarding Council
Member Steve McKee. Specifically, certain details regarding his
contractual relationship with John Verner.

Please consider the following:

The agreement was entered into in 1988. Upon execution of the
agreement, Steve McKee and his father were paid the sum of
$40,000. In accordance with the terms and conditions of the
agreement, in March of 1990, Steve McKee and his father were paid
an additional $25,000. The option was to expire in 1995 or 1996.
That specific information is available int he document of which
you have a copy.

Last year the option agreement was terminated by John Verner.
For your reference, I have enclosed a copy of that document.

I hope the foregoing will be of assistance to you.

Very truly yours,

Law Offices of
WAGGONER & LOEFFLER

CARL O. WAGGONER
CW/jb
Enclosure

01:03:94    12:26    ⊕209 858 5259    CITY OF LATHROP --- CC WAGGONER    ㈜001-001


                                    VERNER CONSTRUCTION
                                    
                                    License No. 321415
                                    2707 East Fremont, Suite 5
                                    STOCKTON, CALIFORNIA 95202
                                    
                                    Telephone (209) 467-1561


                                                                June 8, 1993


Mr. Steven McKee
15440 Ruby Court
Lathrop, California 95330

Re: Joint Venture Agreement dated June 8, 1988

Dear Steve,

Pursuant to the terms and conditions of our Joint Venture Agreement
signed in June of 1988, I have decided to terminate our agreement
as of this date due to economic conditions. Therefore, you are
hereby released from all conditions of the contract as they might
apply, and all monies released as of this date are hereby
forfeited.

It's unfortunate that economic conditions in the San Joaquin Valley
are not better at this time, but since we're several years behind
in our development projections I think this is the best decision
for both of us at this time.

Sincerely,


John T. Verner

Co.    [illegible]        Co.
Dept.                      [illegible]
Fax #                      Phone #
                          Fax #    858-2041

LAW OFFICES
OF
KROLL, LOEFFLER & WAGGONER
611 THIRTEENTH STREET • P.O. BOX 3489
MODESTO, CALIFORNIA 95353-3489

JAMES J. KROLL, JR.
MICHAEL G. LOEFFLER
CAROL O. WAGGONER                                    TELEPHONE (209) 576-7777
                                                     FAX (209) 576-8437
----------

DAVID CERVANTES


August 9, 1990


Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California   95812

RE:  Request for Formal Written Advice/Government Code Section
     83114(b)

Gentlemen:

I have been asked to submit, on behalf of Mayor Stephen McKee, a
request for formal written advice, in accordance with the
provisions of Government Code Section 83114(b).  Please be advised
that for purposes of this request for formal written advice, our
office is the authorized representative.  Mayor McKee's address,
insofar as his official office is concerned, is 16775 Howland Road,
Lathrop, California.  Please consider the following:

Mayor McKee and his father, Robert E. McKee, are the owners of a
ranch located near the corporate limits of the City of Lathrop.
The mayor and his father have entered into an agreement with a
stockton development firm.  For your reference, I have enclosed a
copy of that document.  In accordance with the terms and conditions
of that agreement and addendum thereto, the Mayor and his father
were paid the sum of $25,000.00.  The Stockton development firm,
Verner Construction, has sought to develop property in the City of
Lathrop.  The Mayor abstained from participating in that decision.

The Mayor has since decided not to accept any further money from
Verner Construction for the option to purchase.  The question with
which we are concerned is whether his decision, accompanied by an
appropriate modification of the agreement, would remove any
conflict of interest which would preclude him from participating in
any decision which would have a material financial effect on Verner
Construction.  This is assuming that twelve months had elapsed

Fair Political Practices Commission
August 9, 1990
-Page Two-

since his receipt of the payment of the $25,000.00 had occurred.

For your reference, I have also enclosed two opinions which I
prepared for Mayor McKee regarding this topic.

Should you have any further questions, please contact me. I have
been advised by Mayor McKee that he is more than willing to travel
to your office in Sacramento, to meet with a representative of the
Commission, should clarifications or other information be needed.

Very truly yours,

Law Offices of
KROLL, LOEFFLER & WAGGONER


CARL WAGGONER

Enclosures

cc: City of Lathrop:
    Mayor Stephen McKee
    John Bingham, City Manager

LAW OFFICES
OF
KROLL, LOEFFLER & WAGGONER
611 THIRTEENTH STREET • P.O. BOX 3489
MODESTO, CALIFORNIA 95353-3489

JAMES J. KROLL, JR.
MICHAEL G. LOEFFLER
CAROL O. WAGGONER
                                                    TELEPHONE (209) 576-7777
                                                    FAX (209) 576-8437

DAVID CERVANTES




     Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
     Acting General Counsel
     California Fair Political Practices
     Commission
     428 J Street - Suite 800
     P.O. Box 807
     Sacramento, California  95804-0807

     RE:  Letter No. 90-529/Request for Advice

     Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

     As you are aware, your office is in the process of preparing an
     opinion on certain questions which I have asked on behalf of Mayor
     Steven R. McKee.

     Mayor McKee has requested that I forward to you a statement and
     attachments which he prepared in opposition to a document prepared
     by Beverly Kennedy, a City of Lathrop resident.  Enclosed for your
     review are those documents.

     Mayor McKee believes that these documents may be of assistance to
     you in preparing your opinion, because they shed light on the
     political atmosphere in the City of Lathrop relative to his alleged
     conflict of interest.

     Should you have any questions concerning the foregoing, please
     contact me.

     Very truly yours,

     Law Offices of
     KROLL, LOEFFLER & WAGGONER

     [signature]
     CARL WAGGONER

     CW/mer
     CC:  Mayor Steven McKee

FROM THE DESK OF

STEVEN R. McKEE

Page One of Three

July 25, 1990

For one year now, I have been silent, listening to the repeated accusations of
wrongdoing from Beverly Kennedy concerning my actions as Mayor of the City of
Lathrop. No longer will I remain silent. I feel I must respond. I owe it to
this Community to prove to you the allegations made are false and I can
substantiate my claims with evidence.


Referring to Beverly Kennedy's "Comments for Lathrop General Plan & Related
Development Regulations" dated July 23, 1990, which she submitted for the record
to the Citizens Advisory Committee for the General Plan, accusations have once
again been levied against me and I submit the following to refute her
allegations once and for all.


Reference Paragraph 1 relative to the assertion that I was willing to give
Manteca the Kearny Ventures Project: I submit Exhibits A, B and C proving to
the contrary.


Reference Paragraph 1 relative to the assertion that I attended a meeting with
Kearny Ventures and Manteca Officials prior to Incorporation, I submit that I
was invited to the meeting, as a landowner west of I-5, and elected to attend in
the spirit of cooperation as I have always claimed to be willing to listen to
anyone at anytime. Up until the time I arrived at the meeting, I was not aware
that Manteca Officials would even be present or participating in the meeting.
It is true the Incorporation Committee was not aware of this meeting, however, I
was attending as a landowner and interested party and was not in attendance on
behalf of the Incorporation Committee or anyone else for that matter.


Reference Paragraph 1 relative to the assertion that I claimed my reasons for
running for office was "none of her business". I submit Exhibit D, a letter
from Mr. Jose Perez who was witness to the conversation between myself and Mrs.
Kennedy. Additionally, I made it perfectly clear that I was a farmer, that I
farmed 150 acres in Lathrop in my campaign documents. I never once denied a
conflict and advised all Council Members and City Staff of my potential conflict
and deferred to City Staff for interpretation of Conflict Laws and
Recommendation for those matters from which I must abstain.

Additionally, in a phone conversation with Mrs. Georgina Reichelt, (the "other
person" alluded to) who was also campaign manager for another City Council
Candidate, I advised Mrs. Reichelt that in the event the issue was raised about
my landholdings during the campaign, I certainly would address the issue as I
felt it was no secret to the Community. My wife was a witness to my end of this
phone conversation.

LAW OFFICES
OF
KROLL, LOEFFLER & WAGGONER
611 THIRTEENTH STREET • P.O. BOX 3489
MODESTO, CALIFORNIA 95353-3489

JAMES J. KROLL, JR.                                    TELEPHONE (209) 576-7777
MICHAEL G. LOEFFLER                                    FAX (209) 578-8437
CARL O. WAGGONER

DAVID CERVANTES                          March 14, 1990


Mr. Steven McKee, Mayor
City of Lathrop
Post Office Box 1429
Lathrop, California  95330

Re:  Opinion/Conflict of Interest

Dear Mayor McKee:

     You have asked me for an opinion regarding the issue of whether
you should participate in any decision regarding John Verner, an
individual who is developing a subdivision within the corporate
limits of the City of Lathrop.

     You have informed me that you have a business relationship with
Mr. Verner from the standpoint that he has entered into a sales
contract with you and your father for the purchase of real pro-
perty located outside of the City limits of Lathrop.
Consummation of the sale is dependent upon the property being
annexed to the City.  You and your father, in accordance with
the terms and conditions of the agreement, have recently received
the sum of $25,000 as and for the extension of the agreement.

     It is my conclusion that you should that you should not par-
ticipate in any decision which will have a material financial
effect on Mr. Verner.

     Government Code Section 87100 prohibits, generally, a public
official from participating in any governmental decision in which
he has a financial interest.  Section 87100 provides:

     "    No public official at any level of state or local
     government shall make, participate in making in any way
     attempt to use his official position to influence a
     governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to
     know he has a financial interest."

     To determine whether a financial interest exists, it is necessary
to refer to Government Code Section 87103.  Section 87103 provides
in the pertinent part:

Mr. Steven McKee, Mayor
City of Lathrop
March 14, 1990
Page 2

"    An official has a financial interest in a decision
within the meaning of Section 87100 if it is reasonably
foreseeable that the decision will have a material
financial effect, distinguishable from its effect on the
public generally, on the official or a member or her
immediate family or on:

...

(c)  Any source of income, other than gifts and
other than loans by a commercial lending institution in
the regular course of business on terms available to the
public without regard to official status, aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value pro-
vided to, received by or promised to the public official
within 12 months prior to the time when the decision is
made.

..."

Preliminarily, if it is reasonably foreseeable that participation
in any decision on your part would cause a material financial
effect on the real property you own, then you should abstain from
such action.  Further, as appears in Section 87103 you should not
participate if participation would have a material financial
effect on any source of income.

The question which arises is whether or not John Verner is a
source of income to you.  Government Code Section 82030 defines
income, a copy of which is enclosed for your reference.

As appears in  Sections 82030, income includes "proceeds from any
sale".  Although strictly speaking, the proceeds which you
received were not from the sale of land, it is proceeds from the
sale of a right to purchase.  Therefore, Mr. Verner is a source
of income to you.

I hope the foregoing answered your inquiry.  Should you have any
further questions, please contact me.

Very truly yours,

Law Offices of
KROLL, LOEFFLER & WAGGONER


Carl O. Waggoner

CW:lms
Enclosure

sections.question_synthetic: Whether Mayor McKee can participate in governmental decisions affecting John Verner, a developer with whom McKee has a business relationship involving property sales and option agreements that have generated $25,000-$40,000 in payments to McKee and his father.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Multiple documents bundled together - primarily incoming requests and correspondence rather than FPPC response letters. Document contains attorney opinions, informal advice requests dated January 1994 and August 1990, option agreement termination letter from June 1993, and Mayor's statement from July 1990. No formal FPPC advice letter/conclusion found in this packet. Parsing confidence reduced due to multiple overlapping documents with different dates and purposes.; Date cleared: parsed 1994-01-73 but file year is 1990

embedding.summary: This document package contains correspondence and supporting materials related to FPPC File No. 90-529, concerning potential conflicts of interest for Mayor Stephen McKee of Lathrop regarding his business dealings with developer John Verner. The materials include informal advice requests, formal advice requests, attorney opinions on conflict of interest, termination of the option agreement, and Mayor McKee's response to allegations by a resident.
